Originating in the [Middle Ages](/wiki/Middle_Ages "Middle Ages"), it generally coincided with the affirmation or extension of the city's [civic rights and privileges](/wiki/City_rights_in_the_Low_Countries "City rights in the Low Countries").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Ceremony to celebrate a reigning monarch's first royal entry into a city

For the Joyous Entry of 1356 and the corresponding charter, see [Joyous Entry of 1356](/wiki/Joyous_Entry_of_1356 "Joyous Entry of 1356").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f9/Antoon_van_den_Heuvel_-_Ferdinand_Receives_the_Keys_of_the_City_from_the_Virgin_of_Ghent.jpg/500px-Antoon_van_den_Heuvel_-_Ferdinand_Receives_the_Keys_of_the_City_from_the_Virgin_of_Ghent.jpg)](/wiki/File:Antoon_van_den_Heuvel_-_Ferdinand_Receives_the_Keys_of_the_City_from_the_Virgin_of_Ghent.jpg)

*Ferdinand Receives the [Keys of the City](/wiki/Keys_of_the_City "Keys of the City") from the Virgin of Ghent*, print after a painting made by [Antoon van den Heuvel](/wiki/Antoon_van_den_Heuvel "Antoon van den Heuvel") for the Joyous Entry by the [Cardinal-Infante Ferdinand](/wiki/Cardinal-Infante_Ferdinand "Cardinal-Infante Ferdinand") into Ghent in 1635

A **joyous entry** ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *blijde intrede*; [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *joyeuse entrée*) is a ceremonial event marking the entry into a [city](/wiki/City "City") by a [monarch](/wiki/Monarch "Monarch"), [prince](/wiki/Prince "Prince"), [duke](/wiki/Duke "Duke"), or [governor](/wiki/Governor "Governor") in parts of modern-day [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"). Originating in the [Middle Ages](/wiki/Middle_Ages "Middle Ages"), it generally coincided with the affirmation or extension of the city's [civic rights and privileges](/wiki/City_rights_in_the_Low_Countries "City rights in the Low Countries").

Joyous entries are primarily associated with the historic [Duchy of Brabant](/wiki/Duchy_of_Brabant "Duchy of Brabant") and [County of Flanders](/wiki/County_of_Flanders "County of Flanders") and continue in modern-day Belgium where the most recent joyous entries took place in 2013. The term is also occasionally used in relation to [royal entries](/wiki/Royal_entries "Royal entries") in [Medieval France](/wiki/Medieval_France "Medieval France"), [Luxembourg](/wiki/Luxembourg "Luxembourg"), [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary"), [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland"), and elsewhere.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Montreal-2)[[3]](#cite_note-Leiden-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Ceremonial reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Joyous_entry&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Ceremonial reception")]

A Joyous Entry is a particular form of, and title for, the general phenomenon of ceremonial entries into cities by rulers or their representatives, which were celebrated with enormous [pageantry](/wiki/Medieval_pageant "Medieval pageant") and festivities throughout Europe from at least the late Middle Ages on. The leading artists available designed temporary decorated constructions such as [triumphal arches](/wiki/Triumphal_arch "Triumphal arch"), groups of musicians and actors performed on stands at which the procession halted, the houses on the processional route decorated themselves with hangings, flowers were thrown, and fountains flowed with wine. The custom began in the [Middle Ages](/wiki/Middle_Ages "Middle Ages") and continued until the [French Revolution](/wiki/French_Revolution "French Revolution"), although less often in Protestant counties after the [Reformation](/wiki/Protestant_Reformation "Protestant Reformation"). A formal first visit to a city by an inheritor of the [throne of Belgium](/wiki/Monarchy_of_Belgium "Monarchy of Belgium") upon his accession and since 1900 for a [crown prince](/wiki/Crown_prince "Crown prince") upon his marriage, is still referred to as a "Joyous Entry", a reminder of this tradition of the [rule of law](/wiki/Rechtsstaat "Rechtsstaat").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-Nieuwsbank-6)

## Charter of liberties

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Joyous_entry&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Charter of liberties")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/22/14-4007_Print_Baudartius_Arrival_Don_Juan_in_Brussels_1577_1.jpg/500px-14-4007_Print_Baudartius_Arrival_Don_Juan_in_Brussels_1577_1.jpg)](/wiki/File:14-4007_Print_Baudartius_Arrival_Don_Juan_in_Brussels_1577_1.jpg)

The Joyous Entry of [John of Austria](/wiki/John_of_Austria "John of Austria") into [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels"), 1 May 1577. Print from 'The Wars of Nassau' by [W. Baudartius](/wiki/Willem_Baudartius "Willem Baudartius"), [Amsterdam](/wiki/Amsterdam "Amsterdam") 1616.

In the [Duchy of Brabant](/wiki/Duchy_of_Brabant "Duchy of Brabant") the term Joyous Entry was also applied to the charter of liberties that a new ruler was obliged to swear to uphold upon their formal first reception, dating back to the [Joyous Entry of 1356](/wiki/Joyous_Entry_of_1356 "Joyous Entry of 1356"). One of the functions of the [Council of Brabant](/wiki/Council_of_Brabant "Council of Brabant") was to ensure that new legislation did not contravene or abrogate the liberties established in the Joyous Entry.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Kingdom of Belgium

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Joyous_entry&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Kingdom of Belgium")]

See also: [Swearing-in of the Kings of the Belgians](/wiki/Swearing-in_of_the_Kings_of_the_Belgians "Swearing-in of the Kings of the Belgians")

In Belgium this ceremonial reception of the new sovereign has continued since 1830. Ceremonial entries are performed by the new royal couple in the capitals of the provinces after the installation of the King. The same goes for the Duke of Brabant, who after his marriage presents the new duchess of Brabant to the public. The most recent Joyous Entries were organised in honour of [King Philippe](/wiki/Philippe_of_Belgium "Philippe of Belgium") and [Queen Mathilde](/wiki/Queen_Mathilde_of_Belgium "Queen Mathilde of Belgium") in 2013.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Some notable Joyous Entries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Joyous_entry&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Some notable Joyous Entries")]

* In 1356, the [Joyous Entry into Brussels](/wiki/Joyous_Entry_of_1356 "Joyous Entry of 1356"), by [Joanna](/wiki/Joanna,_Duchess_of_Brabant "Joanna, Duchess of Brabant") and her husband [Wenceslaus I, Duke of Luxembourg](/wiki/Wenceslaus_I,_Duke_of_Luxembourg "Wenceslaus I, Duke of Luxembourg"), upon her becoming Duchess of Brabant on the death of her father [John *the Triumphant*](/wiki/John_III,_Duke_of_Brabant "John III, Duke of Brabant").[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* In 1407?, a Joyous Entry, by [John the Fearless](/wiki/John_the_Fearless "John the Fearless").[[11]](#cite_note-Blockmans-11)
* In 142?, a Joyous Entry, by [Philip the Good](/wiki/Philip_the_Good "Philip the Good").[[11]](#cite_note-Blockmans-11)
* In 1464, the Joyous Entry into [Sopron](/wiki/Sopron "Sopron"), by King [Matthias of Hungary](/wiki/Matthias_Corvinus_of_Hungary "Matthias Corvinus of Hungary"), atypically mainly celebrating the return of the object of the Crown.[[12]](#cite_note-12)
* In 1467, the (not so very) 'Joyous' Entry into [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"), by [Charles the Bold](/wiki/Charles_the_Bold "Charles the Bold").[[11]](#cite_note-Blockmans-11)[[13]](#cite_note-ucla-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)
* In 1468, the Joyous Entry into [Bruges](/wiki/Bruges "Bruges"), by [Charles the Bold](/wiki/Charles_the_Bold "Charles the Bold") and [Margaret of York](/wiki/Margaret_of_York "Margaret of York").[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* In 1478, the Joyous Entry into [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp "Antwerp"), by [Maximilian of Austria](/wiki/Maximilian_I,_Holy_Roman_Emperor "Maximilian I, Holy Roman Emperor").[[3]](#cite_note-Leiden-3)
* In 1493?, the Joyous Entry into [Mechelen](/wiki/Mechelen "Mechelen"), by [Maximilian of Austria](/wiki/Maximilian_I,_Holy_Roman_Emperor "Maximilian I, Holy Roman Emperor") (and his young daughter [Margarete](/wiki/Margaret_of_Austria,_Duchess_of_Savoy "Margaret of Austria, Duchess of Savoy"))
* In 1496, the Joyous Entry into [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels"), by [Joanna *the Mad*](/wiki/Joanna_of_Castile "Joanna of Castile").[[3]](#cite_note-Leiden-3)
* In 1501, a tour of Joyous Entries throughout [Hainaut](/wiki/County_of_Hainaut "County of Hainaut"), [Picardy](/wiki/Picardy "Picardy"), [Île-de-France](/wiki/%C3%8Ele-de-France_(region) "Île-de-France (region)"), [Champagne](/wiki/Champagne_(province) "Champagne (province)"), [Burgundy](/wiki/Burgundy_(region) "Burgundy (region)") and [Franche-Comté](/wiki/Franche-Comt%C3%A9 "Franche-Comté"), by [Philibert II, Duke of Savoy](/wiki/Philibert_II,_Duke_of_Savoy "Philibert II, Duke of Savoy") and [Margarete of Austria](/wiki/Margaret_of_Austria,_Duchess_of_Savoy "Margaret of Austria, Duchess of Savoy") upon their marriage, and the following year into [Bourg-en-Bresse](/wiki/Bourg-en-Bresse "Bourg-en-Bresse").[[2]](#cite_note-Montreal-2)[[16]](#cite_note-JDR-16)
* In 1507, the Joyous Entry into [Mechelen](/wiki/Mechelen "Mechelen"), by [Philibert](/wiki/Philibert_II,_Duke_of_Savoy "Philibert II, Duke of Savoy")'s widow [Margarete](/wiki/Margaret_of_Austria,_Duchess_of_Savoy "Margaret of Austria, Duchess of Savoy"), returning as Regent of the [Low Countries](/wiki/Low_Countries "Low Countries").[[16]](#cite_note-JDR-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)
* In 1515, the Joyous Entries into [Bruges](/wiki/Bruges "Bruges"), [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"), [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp "Antwerp"), and [Leiden](/wiki/Leiden "Leiden"), by young Prince [Charles](/wiki/Charles_V,_Holy_Roman_Emperor "Charles V, Holy Roman Emperor").[[2]](#cite_note-Montreal-2)[[13]](#cite_note-ucla-13)[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/01/Andreas_Martin%2C_De_intrede_te_Brussel_van_aartshertogin_Maria-Elisabeth_%28KMSKB%29.jpg/500px-Andreas_Martin%2C_De_intrede_te_Brussel_van_aartshertogin_Maria-Elisabeth_%28KMSKB%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Andreas_Martin,_De_intrede_te_Brussel_van_aartshertogin_Maria-Elisabeth_(KMSKB).jpg)

*Joyous Entry into Brussels of Archduchess Maria Elisabeth on 9 October 1725* by [Andreas Martin](/wiki/Andreas_Martin_(painter) "Andreas Martin (painter)")

* In 1520, the Joyous Entry into [Bruges](/wiki/Bruges "Bruges"), by young King [Charles](/wiki/Charles_V,_Holy_Roman_Emperor "Charles V, Holy Roman Emperor")
* In 1548, the Joyous Entry into [Lyon](/wiki/Lyon "Lyon"), by [Henri II of Valois](/wiki/Henry_II_of_France "Henry II of France").[[20]](#cite_note-Valois-20)
* In 1549, a series of Joyous Entries into the Low Countries by [Charles V](/wiki/Charles_V,_Holy_Roman_Emperor "Charles V, Holy Roman Emperor") and his son [Philip II of Spain](/wiki/Philip_II_of_Spain "Philip II of Spain") in (among other cities) [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp "Antwerp"), [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels") and [Bruges](/wiki/Bruges "Bruges").[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22)
* In 1550, the Joyous Entry into [Rouen](/wiki/Rouen "Rouen"), by [Henri II of Valois](/wiki/Henry_II_of_France "Henry II of France").[[20]](#cite_note-Valois-20)
* In the early 1560's, the not so very Joyous Entry into [Mechelen](/wiki/Mechelen "Mechelen"), by [Granvelle](/wiki/Antoine_Perrenot_de_Granvelle "Antoine Perrenot de Granvelle"), as [Archbishop](/wiki/Archbishop "Archbishop").[[23]](#cite_note-23)
* In 1577, the Joyous Entry into [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels"), by [Don John](/wiki/John_of_Austria "John of Austria"), as Governor of the [Spanish Netherlands](/wiki/Southern_Netherlands "Southern Netherlands").[[24]](#cite_note-24)
* In 1578, the Joyous Entry into [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels"), by Prince [Matthias, later *the Magnificent*](/wiki/Matthias,_Holy_Roman_Emperor "Matthias, Holy Roman Emperor").[[25]](#cite_note-25)
* In 1582, the Joyous Entry into [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp "Antwerp"), [Bruges](/wiki/Bruges "Bruges"), and [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"), by [François, Duke of Anjou](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois,_Duke_of_Anjou "François, Duke of Anjou").[[13]](#cite_note-ucla-13)
* In 1594, the Joyous Entry into [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels") and [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp "Antwerp"), by [Archduke Ernest of Austria](/wiki/Archduke_Ernest_of_Austria "Archduke Ernest of Austria")
* In 1590, the [Entry into Edinburgh](/wiki/Entry_and_Coronation_of_Anne_of_Denmark "Entry and Coronation of Anne of Denmark"), by [Anne of Denmark](/wiki/Anne_of_Denmark "Anne of Denmark")
* In 1599–1600, a tour of Joyous Entries into [Leuven](/wiki/Leuven "Leuven"), [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels"), [Mechlin](/wiki/Mechlin "Mechlin"), [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp "Antwerp"), [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"), [Bruges](/wiki/Bruges "Bruges"), [Tournai](/wiki/Tournai "Tournai"), etc., by [Archduke Albert](/wiki/Albert_VII,_Archduke_of_Austria "Albert VII, Archduke of Austria") and the [Infanta Isabella](/wiki/Infanta_Isabella_Clara_Eugenia_of_Spain "Infanta Isabella Clara Eugenia of Spain").[[26]](#cite_note-26)[[27]](#cite_note-27)[[28]](#cite_note-dbnl-28)[[29]](#cite_note-29)
* In 1635, the Joyous Entry by the [Cardinal-Infante Ferdinand](/wiki/Cardinal-Infante_Ferdinand "Cardinal-Infante Ferdinand") into Antwerp (decorations designed by [Gaspar Gevartius](/wiki/Gaspar_Gevartius "Gaspar Gevartius"), [Theodoor van Thulden](/wiki/Theodoor_van_Thulden "Theodoor van Thulden") and [Rubens](/wiki/Peter_Paul_Rubens "Peter Paul Rubens")) and Ghent.[[28]](#cite_note-dbnl-28)[[30]](#cite_note-30)[[31]](#cite_note-31)
* On 9 October 1725, [Archduchess Maria Elisabeth of Austria](/wiki/Archduchess_Maria_Elisabeth_of_Austria_(governor) "Archduchess Maria Elisabeth of Austria (governor)") made her Joyous Entry into Brussels as regent governor of the Austrian Netherlands.[[32]](#cite_note-arch-32)
* In 1891, the Joyous Entry into [Luxembourg](/wiki/Luxembourg "Luxembourg"), by [Grand Duke Adolphe](/wiki/Adolphe,_Grand_Duke_of_Luxembourg "Adolphe, Grand Duke of Luxembourg") and his wife Adelheid.[[33]](#cite_note-33)
* On 22 November 1918, [King Albert I](/wiki/Albert_I_of_Belgium "Albert I of Belgium") entered Brussels with the Belgian Army of the Yser after [four years of German occupation](/wiki/German_occupation_of_Belgium_during_World_War_I "German occupation of Belgium during World War I") in [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I").[[34]](#cite_note-34)
